display.purpose: Small app-level titles only. No hero-scale display type in product UI.

body.purpose: Chat summaries, evidence explanations, and memo text.

mono.purpose: Ratios, financial values, JSON, code output, and identifiers.

shadows.name: none-at-rest
shadows.purpose: Default panel and container treatment. Use borders for depth.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus and active control visibility.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, selected, and active state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary command for running the agent or creating work.

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary command for upload and contextual actions.

components.name: Chat Input
components.description: Natural-language prompt input with compact product-control styling.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" placeholder="Ask the agent to analyze a borrower or debt report" />

components.name: Agent Bubble
components.description: Inspectable event in the agent timeline.

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Compact state label for runs, tool events, and covenant decisions.

components.name: Inspector Panel
components.description: Right-side detail surface for selected evidence.
components.html:
Tool call

Input, output, citations, and raw payload for the selected event.

narrative.overview: Vultr-Audit is a product workspace for risk analysts, not a marketing surface. The visual system should feel like a clean digital case file: organized, quiet, source-conscious, and ready for repeated review. The chat is the primary work surface because it shows the investigation unfolding; the inspector and sidebar support the conversation rather than competing with it.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained monochrome surfaces with status color used only for state.
Flat layered panels, borders, and dividers instead of decorative shadows.
Compact shadcn controls with consistent radii and predictable affordances.
Chat-led analysis with inspectable evidence, calculations, and scheduled follow-up work.
Calm copy that explains what the agent is doing in concrete terms.

rules.name: The Status-Only Color Rule
rules.body: Green, amber, red, and blue are state language, not decoration. If a color does not tell the analyst something changed, passed, failed, or is running, remove it.

rules.name: The No Crypto Dark Mode Rule
rules.body: Dark surfaces may exist for code output only. The product shell stays light, clean, and legible.

rules.name: The Flat Case File Rule
rules.body: If a surface can be separated with a 1px border or divider, use that instead of a shadow.

dos: Do lead with the conversation. The chat timeline is the analyst's main work surface.
Do use monochrome surfaces and restrained status colors.
Do keep panels flat and layered with borders.
Do expose source citations, tool inputs, tool outputs, code output, and raw payloads on demand.
donts: Don't make the product look like crypto dark mode, neon fintech, generic AI-purple workflow software, a playful consumer chatbot, or a dense Bloomberg-style terminal.
Don't use marketing-style hero sections, decorative dashboards, or heavy visual effects.
Don't make the main surface a form-heavy interface with many required fields.
Don't hide the paper trail in a final report only. Evidence must be visible where the claim appears.
